data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube's backend is built using a microservices architecture to handle large-scale data and video processing.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services with RESTful APIs, containerized deployments using Kubernetes.

data.title: Distributed Databases
data.description: Utilizes distributed databases to manage massive scale of data and ensure reliability and availability.
data.technical_spec: Google Spanner and Bigtable for storage, sharding and replication strategies.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Uses a global CDN to deliver video content quickly and efficiently to users around the world.
data.technical_spec: Edge caching, regional data centers, adaptive bitrate streaming.

data.title: Load Balancers
data.description: Employs load balancing to distribute incoming network traffic across multiple servers.
data.technical_spec: Layer 7 load balancers, Google Cloud internal systems, dynamic scaling.

data.title: Video Encoding and Transcoding
data.description: Automates the transformation of videos to different formats and qualities.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes ffmpeg libraries, VP9 and AV1 codecs, resolution conversion.
